metadata.name: secret_garden
metadata.description: Secret Garden Protocol — Stage 1 on-chain foundation

docs: Secret Garden Protocol.
Stage 1: game config, player profiles, starter-flower claiming.
Stage 2: flower ownership status + daily competition round lifecycle.
Stage 3A: encrypted breeding — register the `breed` computation definition and
queue breeding computations (the callback that persists results is Stage 3B).

instructions.name: breed_callback
docs: Callback invoked by the Arcium cluster once `breed` finishes.
On success: writes the offspring genome to the pre-created FlowerRecord, commits to
it, flips it Active, unlocks both parents, and Completes the experiment. On failure:
unlocks both parents and marks the experiment Failed (the offspring stays Locked).
Idempotent via `experiment.callback_processed` — a retried callback no-ops.

instructions.name: cancel_expired_experiment
docs: Permissionless recovery: after `EXPERIMENT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS`, anyone can expire a
stuck Queued/Processing experiment to unlock the player's parents. This touches no
Arcium/MPC state. It sets `callback_processed = true`, so if the MPC computation
later completes anyway, `breed_callback`'s idempotency guard makes it a no-op —
preventing a double `active_experiment_count` decrement or a second resolution.
(Trade-off: a successful-but-late computation is discarded; the pre-created
offspring stays Locked. The priority is recovering the player's parent flowers.)

accounts.name: caller
docs: Anyone may call this; the caller only pays the transaction fee.

instructions.name: cancel_stuck_score
docs: Permissionless recovery (Stage 5A): if a scoring computation was queued but its
callback never landed, anyone can reset the entry's in-flight flag after
`SCORE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS` so `queue_score_entry` can be called again. Mirrors
`cancel_expired_experiment`. Nothing is "unlocked" (the entry's flower stays
Submitted regardless), and `round.scored_count` is untouched — it is only ever
incremented by the success callback, so a cancel-then-retry that eventually
succeeds counts exactly once, and one that never succeeds counts zero. Works while
paused: a stuck score must be recoverable even if new game actions are halted.

accounts.name: caller
docs: Anyone may call this; the caller only pays the transaction fee.

instructions.name: claim_starters
docs: Grants the caller their six starter flowers in a single approval. Callable once.

accounts.name: owner
docs: Wallet that owns (and funds) the new flowers.

accounts.name: config
docs: Game config, read to enforce the pause kill-switch.

accounts.name: profile
docs: Caller's profile. Must exist, belong to the signer, and not have claimed yet.
The `starter_claimed` guard is the semantic one-time gate. Note that the six
flower PDAs below are also unique, so a real re-claim additionally collides on
`init` ("account already in use"); both reject the duplicate transaction.

instructions.name: close_round
docs: Closes an Open round (round operator only; may close early or late).

accounts.name: authority
docs: Operator that opened the round.

instructions.name: create_profile
docs: Creates the caller's player profile. Callable once per wallet.

accounts.name: owner
docs: Wallet that owns (and funds) the new profile.

accounts.name: config
docs: Game config, read to enforce the pause kill-switch.

instructions.name: finalize_round
docs: Finalizes a Closed round (round operator only). No scoring in Stage 2.

accounts.name: authority
docs: Operator that opened the round.

instructions.name: init_breeding_comp_def
docs: Registers the `breed` computation definition on-chain. Authority-only, once.

instructions.name: init_reveal_top3_comp_def
docs: Registers the `reveal_top3` computation definition. Authority-only, once.

instructions.name: init_score_entry_comp_def
docs: Registers the `score_entry` computation definition. Authority-only, once.
(Two init instructions because Arcium 0.10.4 binds one accounts struct, via
`#[init_computation_definition_accounts]`, to exactly one circuit — a single
`init_scoring_comp_defs` cannot register both.)

instructions.name: initialize_config
docs: Creates the singleton game config. Callable once.

accounts.name: authority
docs: Authority that funds and administers the game config.

instructions.name: migrate_profile
docs: Stage 5D migration: grows a pre-5D `PlayerProfile` (created with the smaller layout,
before `breeds_this_round`/`last_breed_round` were appended) by 5 bytes so the
current program can read it. Unlike `realloc_flower_genome`, the profile here is
taken as a RAW account: the old layout is 5 bytes short of `PlayerProfile`, so loading
it as `Account<PlayerProfile>` would fail with `AccountDidNotDeserialize` BEFORE any
realloc constraint could run. We grow it in place, preserving the discriminator and
every existing field, and zero-fill the two appended fields. Idempotent (a profile
already at the new size is a no-op) and owner-only (the PDA seeds bind it to the
signer). Runs regardless of the pause kill-switch — it is a recovery/maintenance op.

accounts.name: profile
docs: `PlayerProfile`, so it cannot be loaded as a typed `Account`.

instructions.name: open_round
docs: Opens the next competition round (authority only; previous round must be final).

accounts.name: authority
docs: Configured game authority; funds the new round account.

accounts.name: previous_round
docs: The round at `config.current_round`. Required (and must be Finalized) for every
round after the first; `None` only when `config.current_round == 0`.

instructions.name: queue_reveal_top3
docs: Queues the top-3 reveal for a Closed, fully-scored round. Authority-only.
GAP 2 fix: the encrypted scores are NOT supplied by the caller. The round's
`CompetitionEntry` accounts are passed as `remaining_accounts` (exactly
`participant_count` of them); the program validates each belongs to the round and
is scored, then builds the circuit args by reading each entry's stored score
ciphertext in-place via `ArgBuilder::account()`. Slots beyond `participant_count`
are padded with the first entry's (real, MAC-valid) score, which the circuit masks
to 0 — so a caller can never substitute arbitrary score data.

accounts.name: config
docs: Game config, read to enforce the pause kill-switch (Stage 5A: reveal is game
progression, so it is halted while paused; check added here, logic unchanged).

instructions.name: queue_score_entry
docs: Queues scoring of one entry's flower against the round's public target traits.
Valid once the round is Closed and the entry has NOT already been scored (GAP 1
guard; enforced by the `!entry.scored` constraint on `QueueScoreEntry`). Round
authority signs. The genome is read in-place from the flower account.

accounts.name: config
docs: Game config, read to enforce the pause kill-switch (Stage 5A: scoring is game
progression, so it is halted while paused; check added here, logic unchanged).

accounts.name: flower_record
docs: The entry's flower; its encrypted genome is read in-place by the MPC.

instructions.name: realloc_flower_genome
docs: Grows a `FlowerRecord` to the current (genome-bearing) layout via Anchor's
`realloc` constraint. Flowers created by `claim_starters` are already full size
(Anchor's `Account<FlowerRecord>` requires the full layout to deserialize), so
this is an idempotent, owner-only migration/forward-compatibility safeguard.

instructions.name: reclaim_dead_offspring
docs: Permissionless recovery (Stage 5A): closes the pre-created offspring of a
Failed/Expired breeding and returns its rent to the original player. All validity is
enforced by the `ReclaimDeadOffspring` account constraints (experiment is dead, the
offspring is the Locked flower bound to it both ways, rent destination == owner).
Permissionless is safe because the rent destination is fixed to the flower's owner
regardless of who calls — the caller gains nothing. Works while paused (recovery).

accounts.name: caller
docs: Anyone may call this; the caller gains nothing (rent is fixed to the flower owner).

accounts.name: experiment
docs: The breeding experiment — must be Failed or Expired.

accounts.name: offspring
docs: The pre-created offspring tied to `experiment`. Reclaimable only if it is still
`LOCKED` (a successful breeding would have flipped it `ACTIVE`) AND bound to the
experiment in both directions. `close` returns its rent to `owner_recipient` and
also prevents any double-close (the account no longer exists afterwards).

accounts.name: owner_recipient
docs: Rent destination — must equal the flower's recorded owner (product decision: rent
returns to the player who paid it, not the caller and not the operator).
lamports. Constrained above to equal `offspring.owner`.

instructions.name: reveal_top3_callback
docs: On success: maps each winning SLOT index back to its entry pubkey and writes
top1/top2/top3 — but `top_k` only when `participant_count >= k` (GAP 3). Unfilled
slots stay `Pubkey::default()`, which is unambiguous: a real entry is a program PDA
and can never be at the all-zero default. Sets `scoring_revealed`. Idempotent: a
duplicate callback on an already-revealed round no-ops.

instructions.name: score_entry_callback
docs: On success: persists the entry's encrypted score, marks it `scored`, and bumps
`round.scored_count` (saturating). Idempotent via `entry.scored` — a retried or
raced callback no-ops, which is what makes the GAP 1 double-count structurally
impossible even if `queue_score_entry` were somehow called twice before the first
callback lands. On failure: records a sentinel error_code and leaves `scored =
false` so the entry can be re-queued.

instructions.name: set_paused
docs: Operator kill-switch: sets `GameConfig::paused`. Authority-only (Stage 5A). The
`paused` field has existed since Stage 1 but never had an instruction to set it.

accounts.name: authority
docs: Must equal `config.authority`.

instructions.name: start_breeding
docs: Queues an encrypted breeding computation for the signer's two Active parents and
records the `Experiment`. One wallet approval; the result is handled in Stage 3B.
`env_*` carry the player's private environment encrypted as one
`Enc<Shared, Environment>` (single pubkey + nonce + three `u8` ciphertexts). Each
parent's kind/species/nonce are read from its `FlowerRecord`; the parent genome
ciphertext is referenced in-place from the account (zeroed for Starters).

accounts.name: config
docs: Game config, read to enforce the pause kill-switch (Stage 5A: this player-facing
instruction previously had no pause gate — added here, logic otherwise unchanged).

accounts.name: offspring
docs: Offspring flower, pre-created here (Arcium callbacks cannot init accounts). Its
index is the wallet's running `total_flowers` (starters occupy 0..=5). The genome
is written by `breed_callback`.

instructions.name: submit_entry
docs: Submits one Active flower as an entry into an Open round.

accounts.name: player
docs: The player submitting the entry; funds the entry account.

accounts.name: config
docs: Game config, read to enforce the pause kill-switch (Stage 5A: this player-facing
instruction previously had no pause gate — added here, logic otherwise unchanged).

accounts.name: round
docs: Target round. The seed check ties the passed account to its stored `round_id`.

accounts.name: flower_record
docs: Flower being submitted. Ownership and status are validated in the handler.

errors.name: AlreadyInitialized
errors.msg: Game config has already been initialized

errors.name: NotAuthority
errors.msg: Signer is not the configured authority

errors.name: GamePaused
errors.msg: The game is currently paused

errors.name: ProfileAlreadyExists
errors.msg: A profile already exists for this wallet

errors.name: StartersAlreadyClaimed
errors.msg: Starter flowers have already been claimed

errors.name: InvalidSpecies
errors.msg: Species index is out of range

errors.name: PreviousRoundNotFinalized
errors.msg: The previous round has not been finalized

errors.name: RoundNotOpen
errors.msg: The round is not open

errors.name: RoundDeadlinePassed
errors.msg: The round deadline has passed

errors.name: RoundFull
errors.msg: The round is full

errors.name: FlowerNotOwned
errors.msg: The flower is not owned by the signer

errors.name: FlowerNotActive
errors.msg: The flower is not active

errors.name: RoundNotClosed
errors.msg: The round is not closed

errors.name: ParentsMustBeDistinct
errors.msg: The two parents must be distinct flowers

errors.name: AbortedComputation
errors.msg: The computation was aborted

errors.name: ExperimentNotYetExpired
errors.msg: The experiment has not yet expired

errors.name: ExperimentAlreadyResolved
errors.msg: The experiment has already been resolved

errors.name: ScoringIncomplete
errors.msg: Not all entries have been scored yet

errors.name: ScoringAlreadyRevealed
errors.msg: Scoring has already been revealed

errors.name: EntryAlreadyScored
errors.msg: This entry has already been scored

errors.name: WrongEntryCount
errors.msg: Wrong number of entry accounts for the round

errors.name: ScoreAlreadyQueued
errors.msg: A scoring computation is already in flight for this entry

errors.name: ScoreNotQueued
errors.msg: The entry is not currently queued for scoring

errors.name: ScoreNotYetTimedOut
errors.msg: The scoring computation has not yet timed out

errors.name: ExperimentNotDead
errors.msg: The experiment is not in a failed or expired state

errors.name: OffspringNotReclaimable
errors.msg: The offspring is not a reclaimable dead flower for this experiment

errors.name: InvalidRentDestination
errors.msg: The rent destination must be the flower owner

errors.name: BreedingLimitReached
errors.msg: You have used all 5 breeding attempts for this round

types.name: BreedOutput
docs: The output of the callback instruction. Provided as a struct with ordered fields
as anchor does not support tuples and tuple structs yet.

types.name: BreedingComputedEvent
docs: Emitted by `breed_callback` when a breeding computation succeeds.

fields.name: ciphertexts
docs: The offspring genome ciphertext (10 scalars * 32 bytes).

fields.name: nonce
docs: The MXE nonce (little-endian u128).

types.name: ClockAccount
docs: An account storing the current network epoch

types.name: CompetitionEntry
docs: A player's entry into a round. PDA seeds: `[b"entry", round, player]`.
The PDA is unique per (round, player), so the `init` constraint failing on a second
submission is itself the duplicate-entry guard — no manual check is needed.

fields.name: round
docs: The `CompetitionRound` this entry belongs to.

fields.name: player
docs: The player that submitted the entry.

fields.name: flower_record
docs: The `FlowerRecord` submitted to the round.

fields.name: submitted_at
docs: Unix timestamp the entry was submitted.

fields.name: status
docs: Entry status (see `ENTRY_STATUS_*`). Stage 2 only sets `SUBMITTED`.

fields.name: bump
docs: PDA bump.

fields.name: encrypted_score
docs: `Enc<Mxe, u8>` score ciphertext (zero until scored). Read in-place by
`reveal_top3` via `ArgBuilder::account()` — the integrity fix that stops callers
from supplying fabricated scores.

fields.name: score_nonce
docs: MXE nonce for `encrypted_score` (little-endian u128).

fields.name: scored
docs: True once `score_entry_callback` has persisted this entry's score. Gates re-queuing
(`queue_score_entry` requires `scored == false`) and makes the callback idempotent.

fields.name: score_error_code
docs: Failure code (0 = none); set by `score_entry_callback` on a failed computation.

fields.name: score_queued
docs: True while a scoring computation is in flight. Set by `queue_score_entry`; cleared
by `score_entry_callback` (on success OR failure) and by `cancel_stuck_score`. Acts
as the "currently queued" state: it blocks a second concurrent queue and is what
`cancel_stuck_score` resets so a stuck (never-callback'd) entry becomes re-queueable.

fields.name: queued_at
docs: Unix timestamp of the most recent `queue_score_entry` for this entry (0 until first
queued). Drives the `cancel_stuck_score` timeout.

types.name: CompetitionRound
docs: A daily competition round. PDA seeds: `[b"round", round_id_le]`.

fields.name: round_id
docs: Monotonic round number (== `GameConfig::current_round` at open time).

fields.name: status
docs: Lifecycle status (see `ROUND_STATUS_*`).

fields.name: start_time
docs: Unix timestamp the round opened.

fields.name: end_time
docs: Submission deadline: `start_time + ROUND_DURATION_SECONDS`.

fields.name: max_participants
docs: Maximum number of entries allowed (see `MAX_PARTICIPANTS`).

fields.name: participant_count
docs: Number of entries submitted so far.

fields.name: authority
docs: Operator that opened the round; the only signer allowed to close/finalize it.

fields.name: bump
docs: PDA bump.

fields.name: target_traits
docs: Public target trait ids for this round (see `TRAIT_TABLE`); only the first
`target_trait_count` slots are active. Generated at `open_round` time.

fields.name: target_trait_count
docs: Number of active trait slots (`TARGET_TRAIT_MIN..=TARGET_TRAIT_MAX`).

fields.name: top1
docs: Winner `CompetitionEntry` pubkeys, `Pubkey::default()` until Stage 4B's
`reveal_top3` callback fills them.

fields.name: scoring_revealed
docs: False until Stage 4B finalizes results.

fields.name: scored_count
docs: Count of entries scored so far. Incremented by Stage 4B's `score_entry` callback
(not written in Stage 4A); gates `queue_reveal_top3`.

types.name: ComputationDefinitionAccount
docs: An account representing a [ComputationDefinition] in a MXE.

types.name: ComputationDefinitionMeta
docs: A computation definition for execution in a MXE.

types.name: ComputationSignature
docs: The signature of a computation defined in a [ComputationDefinition].

types.name: Epoch
docs: The network epoch

types.name: Experiment
docs: A breeding experiment: one queued (and later resolved) MPC computation.
PDA seeds: `[b"experiment", owner, experiment_index_le]` where `experiment_index`
is `PlayerProfile::total_experiments` at creation time.

fields.name: owner
docs: Wallet that started the experiment.

fields.name: parent_a
docs: First parent flower.

fields.name: parent_b
docs: Second parent flower.

fields.name: computation_offset
docs: Arcium computation offset for this experiment's queued computation.

fields.name: status
docs: Lifecycle status (see `EXPERIMENT_STATUS_*`). Stage 3A only sets `QUEUED`.

fields.name: result_flower
docs: Offspring flower, written by Stage 3B's callback (`Pubkey::default()` until then).

fields.name: created_at
docs: Unix timestamp the experiment was created.

fields.name: updated_at
docs: Unix timestamp of the last status change.

fields.name: error_code
docs: Failure code (0 = none); set by Stage 3B on failure/expiry.

fields.name: callback_processed
docs: Whether Stage 3B's callback has already processed this experiment.

fields.name: bump
docs: PDA bump.

types.name: FlowerRecord
docs: One record per flower a wallet owns. PDA seeds: `[b"flower", owner, flower_index_le]`.
NOTE: Stage 1 deliberately stores NO genome / commitment / ciphertext. Stage 3 will
realloc this account to append encrypted-genome data once the Arcium circuit fixes
the ciphertext size. `genome_status` already distinguishes Starter (0) from
Encrypted (1) so client reload logic remains stable across stages.

fields.name: owner
docs: Wallet that owns this flower.

fields.name: flower_index
docs: Index of this flower within the owner's collection (also a PDA seed).

fields.name: visual_species_id
docs: Cosmetic species id used by the client renderer.

fields.name: generation
docs: Breeding generation (0 for starters).

fields.name: rarity
docs: Rarity tier (see `RARITY_*`).

fields.name: stability
docs: Genetic stability on a 0..=100 scale (100 for starters).

fields.name: revealed_trait_mask
docs: Bitmask of publicly revealed cosmetic traits (see `TRAIT_*`).

fields.name: parent_a
docs: First parent flower (default/zero for starters).

fields.name: parent_b
docs: Second parent flower (default/zero for starters).

fields.name: genome_status
docs: Genome lifecycle marker (see `GENOME_STATUS_*`).

fields.name: source_experiment
docs: Source breeding experiment (default/zero for starters).

fields.name: status
docs: Lifecycle status (see `FLOWER_STATUS_*`).

fields.name: created_at
docs: Unix timestamp the flower was created.

fields.name: bump
docs: PDA bump.

fields.name: genome_commitment
docs: Hash commitment to `encrypted_genome` (zero until a genome is attached).

fields.name: encrypted_genome
docs: `Enc<Mxe, Genome>` ciphertext: 10 scalars * 32 bytes (see ENCRYPTED_GENOME_LEN).

fields.name: encryption_metadata
docs: MXE nonce for `encrypted_genome` (little-endian u128 = 16 bytes).

types.name: GameConfig
docs: Singleton game configuration. PDA seeds: `[b"config"]`.

fields.name: authority
docs: Wallet allowed to administer the game (set at initialization).

fields.name: paused
docs: Global kill-switch; when `true`, player-facing instructions are rejected.

fields.name: current_round
docs: Current game round counter (advanced by later stages).

fields.name: starter_count
docs: Number of starter flowers granted by `claim_starters`.

fields.name: version
docs: On-chain schema version (see `PROGRAM_VERSION`).

fields.name: bump
docs: PDA bump.

types.name: MXEAccount
docs: A MPC Execution Environment.

types.name: MxeStatus
docs: The status of an MXE.

types.name: NodeMetadata
docs: location as [ISO 3166-1 alpha-2](https://www.iso.org/iso-3166-country-codes.html) country code

types.name: NodeRef
docs: A reference to a node in the cluster.
The offset is to derive the Node Account.
The current_total_rewards is the total rewards the node has received so far in the current
epoch.

types.name: Output
docs: An output of a computation.
We currently don't support encrypted outputs yet since encrypted values are passed via
data objects.

types.name: Parameter
docs: A parameter of a computation.
We differentiate between plaintext and encrypted parameters and data objects.
Plaintext parameters are directly provided as their value.
Encrypted parameters are provided as an offchain reference to the data.
Data objects are provided as a reference to the data object account.

types.name: PlayerProfile
docs: Per-wallet player profile. PDA seeds: `[b"profile", owner]`.

fields.name: owner
docs: Wallet that owns this profile.

fields.name: starter_claimed
docs: Whether this wallet has already claimed its starter flowers.

fields.name: total_flowers
docs: Total flowers owned (6 immediately after claiming starters).

fields.name: total_crosses
docs: Total successful crosses performed (Stage 2+).

fields.name: daily_attempts
docs: Breeding attempts used in the current day window (Stage 2+).

fields.name: final_submissions
docs: Final submissions made to a challenge (Stage 4+).

fields.name: created_at
docs: Unix timestamp the profile was created.

fields.name: active_experiment_count
docs: Breeding experiments currently in flight. Incremented by `start_breeding`
(Stage 3A); decremented when an experiment resolves to Completed/Expired
(Stage 3B's callback / cancel instructions).

fields.name: total_experiments
docs: Monotonic count of experiments ever started; never decremented. Used as the
`experiment_index` nonce in the `Experiment` PDA so a wallet can run many
concurrent experiments without seed collisions.

fields.name: next_flower_index
docs: Monotonic next FlowerRecord index (PDA nonce). Starters occupy 0..=5, so this is
`STARTER_COUNT` after claiming. A dedicated `u32` (rather than the `u16`
`total_flowers`) keeps the flower PDA seed a clean 4-byte index and avoids a cast
in the seed (which the IDL builder rejects).

fields.name: bump
docs: PDA bump.

fields.name: breeds_this_round
docs: `start_breeding` attempts used in the round identified by `last_breed_round`
(0..=`MAX_BREEDS_PER_ROUND`). Reset to 0 lazily on the first breed of a new round.

fields.name: last_breed_round
docs: The `GameConfig::current_round` (truncated to `u32`) the player last bred in. When
this differs from the live `current_round`, `breeds_this_round` is stale and resets.

types.name: RevealTop3Output
docs: The output of the callback instruction. Provided as a struct with ordered fields
as anchor does not support tuples and tuple structs yet.

types.name: ScoreComputedEvent
docs: Emitted by the Stage 4A `score_entry` callback stub once a score verifies.

fields.name: ciphertext
docs: The encrypted score ciphertext (1 scalar * 32 bytes).

fields.name: nonce
docs: The MXE nonce (little-endian u128).

types.name: ScoreEntryOutput
docs: The output of the callback instruction. Provided as a struct with ordered fields
as anchor does not support tuples and tuple structs yet.

types.name: SetUnset
docs: Utility struct to store a value that needs to be set by a certain number of participants (keys
in our case). Once all participants have set the value, the value is considered set and we only
store it once.

types.name: Top3RevealedEvent
docs: Emitted by the Stage 4A `reveal_top3` callback stub. The winners are public.
